description:
Goal: make `dispatch_matrix` a genuinely shared compiler service that Compiler2 can consume directly, without copying the planner and without adapting Compiler2 through old-world compatibility glue.

Change:
- Neutralize the remaining old-world handle leakage at the shared planner boundary so producers identify subjects/inputs without `fz_ir::Var` baggage.
- Keep the planner and its algorithm in one place; old-world and Compiler2 consumers both adapt at their own edges to the same shared core.
- Move any old-consumer-specific assumptions out of the shared plan artifact rather than wrapping the artifact in Compiler2-local adapters.
- Preserve and extend the existing `dispatch_matrix` tests in place as the canonical contract for ordered dispatch planning.

Why:
- Copying the planner into Compiler2 would fork semantics and invite drift.
- A bridge layer that pretends Compiler2 is the old lowerer would be locally convenient and globally wrong.
- The clean solution is one shared planner with a producer-neutral surface.

Checks:
- Existing `dispatch_matrix` tests still pass after the boundary neutralization.
- Old consumers still compile against the shared planner through their own edge logic.
- Compiler2 can construct planner inputs directly, with no compatibility wrapper module and no planner clone.

Non-goals:
- No Compiler2 semantic facts yet.
- No Compiler2-local import or fork of `dispatch_matrix`.
- No temporary adapter layer translating Compiler2 data into a fake old-world shape.
title: Compiler2 dispatch: neutralize shared dispatch_matrix for cross-compiler reuse
